title: styphelia psammophila
intro: Styphelia psammophila, also known as the sand heath, is a small shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Styphelia psammophila are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Styphelia psammophila can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
